channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-8] Use non-blocking connect in ksocklnd
item.description: Related to bz 14846.

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment: Landed for 1.8.6 and 2.1

Integrated in lustre-master » x86_64,server,el5,ofa #148

Integrated in reviews-centos5 #633
LU-8 dynamically grow/shrink socklnd connd threads

Liang Zhen : 26ced7685db081733e7ebbae34606b50537d2d6f
Files :

  • lnet/klnds/socklnd/socklnd_modparams.c
  • lnet/klnds/socklnd/socklnd.h

  • lnet/klnds/socklnd/socklnd_cb.c

Patch landed on 1.8.*
I will port it to 2.* and push it to upstream
I am sorry, I have not had the time to test this.

Chris, have you got any chance to try this patch? I'm thinking about push it into upstream if possible.

Thanks
Liang

Chris, I've updated the patch, briefly I just removed the interval for creating new thread. I didn't reserve more threads for creating new threads, because we have already reserved one thread for accepting, which is unlikely to be blocked for long time (the worst case is 10 seconds, which is unlikely to happen). But I can change it to have a dedicated thread for creating new thread if you still think it's necessary.

Liang
I added a comment on the patch in gerrit.
Status: Next step is for LLNL to test in their environment.
Liang, can you put a short comment in bz 14846 just to say you are working on a related fix and give the Jira pointer to this bug. Thanks.
I spoke with Chris yesterday to sort out the issues with Gerrit. He's going to pull the patch and try this on his tree next.

Robert, I am finding the gerrit page a bit confusing. It appears from the comments at the bottom that I only want patch set 2, but there appears to be no way from the information at the top to discern that fact.

It gives every appearance that both patch sets are approved as a whole.

Chris, the patch has been inspected and Liang has tested it as much as he can, so it seems we're ready to pass it on to you guys (and also to Oracle). You can pull the 1.8-based
patch directly from Gerrit (the review link above has instructions for several methods of downloading the patch), or, if there are merge issues on your end, we can add a mirror of your chaos tree to review.whamcloud.com and merge the patch onto chaos here. (I do intend to mirror your tree eventually, but things are bit busy right now with SC coming up.)

Please let me or Dan know if that works for you or if you would like to manage this differently.

I've made some changes due to inspection, second version of patch is here: http://review.whamcloud.com/#change,119

Liang

First version of patch

http://review.whamcloud.com/#change,119

Liang

I think suggestion from Andreas is quite reasonable, we can make connd threads-pool increase & shrink automatically (just like ptlrpc service threads)

  • if there is too many active connecting requests, create more threads
  • if number of connecting requests is under a low water, we can kill some connds

Christopher, if you think it's acceptable, then I can start very soon.

Regards
Liang
Are you really planning on making the connect non-blocking, or just implement dynamic creation of ksocknal_connd() threads as Andreas suggests?
They are running 1.8.3 and soon moving to 1.8.4, so you are right - they would like it based on the newer release. Dan.

Robert,
which branch should it base on? 14846 is created for 1.6, but I guess we want it on newer version right?

Liang
